content.Text:
[Thu 2026-04-23 00:21 PDT] Create a new file `governance.html` - an "Agent Governance Dashboard" page for the AgentBox marketing/demo site.

## Style Requirements
Follow the exact same dark theme CSS variables and conventions used in other pages like capability-matrix.html:
- Dark theme with: --bg: #0a0a0f; --surface: #12121a; --surface2: #1a1a2e; --border: #2a2a3e; --text: #e0e0e8; --muted: #8888aa; --accent: #6c5ce7; --accent2: #a29bfe; --green: #00b894; --yellow: #fdcb6e; --red: #e17055; --blue: #74b9ff
- Font: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif
- Self-contained single HTML file, no external dependencies
- Header with title "Agent Governance Dashboard" and subtitle
- Tab-based navigation between panels
- Back link to index.html

## Feature: Agent Governance Dashboard
An interactive governance policy builder and compliance monitor with 5 tabs:

### Tab 1: Policy Builder
- Interactive form to create governance policies
- Policy types: Approval Required, Rate Limit, Escalation Rule, Data Access, Budget Cap, Kill Switch
- Each policy has: name, type, description, severity (Critical/High/Medium/Low), scope (All Agents/Specific Agent/Agent Group), conditions (configurable thresholds), enabled toggle
- Pre-loaded with 8 example policies (e.g., "Human approval for external API calls", "Rate limit: max 100 requests/min", "Auto-escalate on 3 consecutive failures", "Budget cap: $50/day per agent", "Kill switch on safety score < 40")
- Add/edit/delete policies
- Policy cards with color-coded severity badges
- Search/filter by type and severity

### Tab 2: Approval Workflow
- Visual approval chain builder
- Show approval flow: Agent Request → Policy Check → Auto-approve / Requires Approval → Reviewer(s) → Approved/Denied
- 5 preset workflow templates (Standard, High Security, Fast Track, Emergency, Audit-Heavy)
- Interactive: click nodes to configure, drag to reorder
- Animated flow visualization showing a request traveling through the chain
- Stats: avg approval time, auto-approve rate, denial rate

### Tab 3: Audit Trail
- Simulated audit log table with 50+ entries auto-generated
- Columns: Timestamp, Agent, Action, Policy, Decision (Approved/Denied/Escalated/Auto-approved), Reviewer, Duration
- Filters: by decision, by agent, by policy, date range
- Color-coded decision badges
- Click row for detail modal
- Export as JSON/CSV

### Tab 4: Compliance Score
- Overall compliance gauge (Canvas ring chart, 0-100)
- 6 compliance dimensions: Policy Coverage, Enforcement Rate, Audit Completeness, Response Time, Violation Resolution, Documentation
- Radar chart (Canvas) showing all 6 dimensions
- Per-policy compliance breakdown with bar charts
- Trend line showing compliance over last 30 days
- Grade letter (A+ to F) based on score

### Tab 5: Autonomous Advisor (Agentic Feature)
- "Governance AI" that analyzes policies and audit trail
- Auto-generates recommendations:
  - "Policy gap detected: No rate limit on Agent-7" 
  - "Approval bottleneck: Reviewer X avg 4.2h response time"
  - "Violation trend: 3x increase in budget overruns this week"
  - "Suggested policy: Add kill switch for Agent-12 (safety score declining)"
  - "Auto-approve candidate: 98% of Agent-3 data access requests approved"
- Recommendations have severity, confidence score, "Apply" button
- Auto-monitor toggle that periodically re-analyzes (simulated with setInterval)
- Proactive alerts that pulse/animate when new issues detected
- Summary stats: policies analyzed, gaps found, recommendations applied

Make it fully interactive with simulated data. All Canvas charts should render properly. The page should feel like a real governance tool demo. Around 800-1200 lines of code total.

Also update `index.html` - find the existing link section/grid and add a link card for "Agent Governance Dashboard" pointing to governance.html. Match the existing card style. Look for where other tool links like "capability-matrix.html", "trust-score.html" etc are listed and add it there.

IMPORTANT: Do NOT use any git commands. Just create/edit the files.

Falling back from WebSockets to HTTPS transport. stream disconnected before completion: An error occurred while processing your request. You can retry your request, or contact us through our help center at help.openai.com if the error persists. Please include the request ID 25069659-52b3-450f-8f56-af6d4d92c4c0 in your message.
